003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.collections;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.stream.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
026
027/**
028 * A composite map that presents multiple maps as a single unified map.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * This class allows multiple maps to be viewed and accessed as if they were merged into
032 * a single map, without actually copying the entries. When the same key exists in multiple maps,
033 * the value from the first map (in constructor order) is returned.
034 *
035 * <h5 class='section'>Features:</h5>
036 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
037 *    <li><b>Zero-Copy Composition:</b> No data is copied when creating a MultiMap; it simply wraps the provided maps
038 *    <li><b>Transparent Access:</b> Accessing entries by key seamlessly searches all underlying maps in order
039 *    <li><b>First-Wins Semantics:</b> When a key exists in multiple maps, the value from the first map is returned
040 *    <li><b>Modification Support:</b> Entries can be removed via the iterator's {@link Iterator#remove()} method
041 *    <li><b>Efficient Size Calculation:</b> The size is computed by counting unique keys across all maps
042 * </ul>
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
045 * <p class='bjava'>
046 *    <jc>// Create a MultiMap from three separate maps</jc>
047 *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map1</jv> = Map.of(<js>"key1"</js>, <js>"value1"</js>, <js>"key2"</js>, <js>"value2"</js>);
048 *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map2</jv> = Map.of(<js>"key3"</js>, <js>"value3"</js>, <js>"key2"</js>, <js>"value2b"</js>);
049 *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map3</jv> = Map.of(<js>"key4"</js>, <js>"value4"</js>);
050 *
051 *    MultiMap&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>multiMap</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MultiMap&lt;&gt;(<jv>map1</jv>, <jv>map2</jv>, <jv>map3</jv>);
052 *
053 *    <jc>// Access entries by key</jc>
054 *    <jv>multiMap</jv>.get(<js>"key1"</js>);  <jc>// Returns "value1" from map1</jc>
055 *    <jv>multiMap</jv>.get(<js>"key2"</js>);  <jc>// Returns "value2" from map1 (first match wins)</jc>
056 *    <jv>multiMap</jv>.get(<js>"key3"</js>);  <jc>// Returns "value3" from map2</jc>
057 *
058 *    <jc>// Iterate over all entries from all maps</jc>
059 *    <jk>for</jk> (Map.Entry&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>entry</jv> : <jv>multiMap</jv>.entrySet()) {
060 *       System.<jsf>out</jsf>.println(<jv>entry</jv>); <jc>// Prints entries from all maps</jc>
061 *    }
062 *
063 *    <jc>// Get total size (unique keys across all maps)</jc>
064 *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>totalSize</jv> = <jv>multiMap</jv>.size(); <jc>// Returns: 4 (key1, key2, key3, key4)</jc>
065 * </p>
066 *
067 * <h5 class='section'>Behavior Notes:</h5>
068 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
069 *    <li>The order of key lookup follows the order of maps as provided in the constructor
070 *    <li>When a key exists in multiple maps, {@link #get(Object)} returns the value from the first map containing that key
071 *    <li>The underlying maps must not be <jk>null</jk>, but can be empty
072 *    <li>Modifications via {@link Iterator#remove()} are delegated to the underlying map's entry set iterator
073 *    <li>This class does not support {@link #put(Object, Object)}, {@link #remove(Object)}, or {@link #clear()} operations
074 *    <li>The {@link #size()} method recomputes the count of unique keys each time it's called (not cached)
075 *    <li>The {@link #entrySet()} iterator only returns each key once (first occurrence), even if it exists in multiple maps
076 * </ul>
077 *
078 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
079 * <p>
080 * This class is not inherently thread-safe. If the underlying maps are modified concurrently
081 * during iteration or access, the behavior is undefined. Synchronization must be handled externally if needed.
082 *
083 * <h5 class='section'>Example - Processing Multiple Configuration Sources:</h5>
084 * <p class='bjava'>
085 *    <jc>// Combine configuration from system properties, environment, and defaults</jc>
086 *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>systemProps</jv> = getSystemProperties();
087 *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>envVars</jv> = getEnvironmentVariables();
088 *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>defaults</jv> = getDefaultConfig();
089 *
090 *    MultiMap&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>config</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MultiMap&lt;&gt;(<jv>systemProps</jv>, <jv>envVars</jv>, <jv>defaults</jv>);
091 *
092 *    <jc>// Access configuration (system props take precedence, then env vars, then defaults)</jc>
093 *    String <jv>host</jv> = <jv>config</jv>.get(<js>"host"</js>);
094 * </p>
095 *
096 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5>
097 * <ul>
098 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="../../../../../index.html#juneau-commons">Overview &gt; juneau-commons</a>
099 * </ul>
100 *
101 * @param <K> The key type of this map.
102 * @param <V> The value type of this map.
103 */
104@SuppressWarnings({
105   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
106   "java:S3740"  // Raw Map/Iterator types used in multi-map structural operations where value collection type is erased
107})
108public class MultiMap<K,V> extends AbstractMap<K,V> {
109
110   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
111   private static final String ARG_maps = "maps";
112
113   /**
114    * The underlying maps being wrapped by this MultiMap.
115    * <p>
116    * These maps are accessed directly during key lookup and iteration without copying.
117    */
118   final Map<K,V>[] m;
119
120   /**
121    * Creates a new MultiMap that presents the specified maps as a single unified map.
122    *
123    * <p>
124    * The maps are stored by reference (not copied), so modifications made through the
125    * MultiMap's iterator will affect the original maps.
126    *
127    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
128    * <p class='bjava'>
129    *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map1</jv> = <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;(Map.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"1"</js>));
130    *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map2</jv> = <jk>new</jk> LinkedHashMap&lt;&gt;(Map.of(<js>"b"</js>, <js>"2"</js>));
131    *
132    *    MultiMap&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>multiMap</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MultiMap&lt;&gt;(<jv>map1</jv>, <jv>map2</jv>);
133    *    <jc>// multiMap now represents all entries from both maps</jc>
134    * </p>
135    *
136    * @param maps Zero or more maps to combine into this map. Must not be <jk>null</jk>,
137    *           and no individual map can be <jk>null</jk> (but maps can be empty).
138    * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the maps array or any map within it is <jk>null</jk>.
139    */
140   @SafeVarargs
141   public MultiMap(Map<K,V>...maps) {
142      assertArgNotNull(ARG_maps, maps);
143      for (var map : maps)
144         assertArgNotNull(ARG_maps, map);
145      m = maps;
146   }
147
148   /**
149    * Returns the value to which the specified key is mapped, or <jk>null</jk> if this map contains no mapping for the key.
150    *
151    * <p>
152    * This method searches the underlying maps in the order they were provided to the constructor.
153    * The first map containing the key determines the returned value.
154    *
155    * @param key The key whose associated value is to be returned.
156    * @return The value to which the specified key is mapped, or <jk>null</jk> if this map contains no mapping for the key.
157    */
158   @Override
159   public V get(Object key) {
160      for (var map : m) {
161         if (map.containsKey(key))
162            return map.get(key);
163      }
164      return null;
165   }
166
167   /**
168    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this map contains a mapping for the specified key.
169    *
170    * @param key The key whose presence in this map is to be tested.
171    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this map contains a mapping for the specified key.
172    */
173   @Override
174   public boolean containsKey(Object key) {
175      for (var map : m) {
176         if (map.containsKey(key))
177            return true;
178      }
179      return false;
180   }
181
182   /**
183    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this map maps one or more keys to the specified value.
184    *
185    * @param value The value whose presence in this map is to be tested.
186    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this map maps one or more keys to the specified value.
187    */
188   @Override
189   public boolean containsValue(Object value) {
190      for (var map : m) {
191         if (map.containsValue(value))
192            return true;
193      }
194      return false;
195   }
196
197   /**
198    * Returns a {@link Set} view of the mappings contained in this map.
199    *
200    * <p>
201    * The returned set is a composite view of all underlying maps. When a key exists in multiple maps,
202    * only the entry from the first map (in constructor order) is included.
203    *
204    * <p>
205    * The iterator supports the {@link Iterator#remove()} operation, which removes the entry
206    * from its underlying map.
207    *
208    * @return A set view of the mappings contained in this map.
209    */
210   @Override
211   @SuppressWarnings({
212      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for entry set iteration over multiple maps
213   })
214   public Set<Entry<K,V>> entrySet() {
215      return new AbstractSet<>() {
216         @Override
217         public Iterator<Entry<K,V>> iterator() {
218            return new EntryIterator();
219         }
220
221         @Override
222         public int size() {
223            return MultiMap.this.size();
224         }
225      };
226   }
227
228   private final class EntryIterator implements Iterator<Entry<K,V>> {
229      int mapIndex;
230      Iterator<Entry<K,V>> currentIterator;
231      final Set<K> seenKeys = new HashSet<>();
232      Entry<K,V> nextEntry;
233      Iterator<Entry<K,V>> lastIterator;
234      boolean canRemove;
235
236      EntryIterator() {
237         mapIndex = 0;
238         currentIterator = null;
239         nextEntry = null;
240         lastIterator = null;
241         canRemove = false;
242         if (m.length > 0) {
243            currentIterator = m[0].entrySet().iterator();
244            advance();
245         }
246      }
247
248      private void advance() {
249         nextEntry = null;
250         while (currentIterator != null) {
251            while (currentIterator.hasNext()) {
252               var entry = currentIterator.next();
253               if (!seenKeys.contains(entry.getKey())) {
254                  seenKeys.add(entry.getKey());
255                  nextEntry = entry;
256                  return;
257               }
258            }
259            mapIndex++;
260            if (mapIndex < m.length) {
261               currentIterator = m[mapIndex].entrySet().iterator();
262            } else {
263               currentIterator = null;
264            }
265         }
266      }
267
268      @Override
269      public boolean hasNext() {
270         return nextEntry != null;
271      }
272
273      @Override
274      public Entry<K,V> next() {
275         if (nextEntry == null)
276            throw new NoSuchElementException();
277         var result = nextEntry;
278         lastIterator = currentIterator;
279         canRemove = true;
280         advance();
281         return result;
282      }
283
284      @Override
285      public void remove() {
286         if (!canRemove || lastIterator == null)
287            throw new IllegalStateException();
288         lastIterator.remove();
289         canRemove = false;
290      }
291   }
292
293   /**
294    * Returns the number of unique key-value mappings in this map.
295    *
296    * <p>
297    * This method computes the size by counting unique keys across all underlying maps.
298    * If a key exists in multiple maps, it is counted only once. The size is recalculated
299    * each time this method is called (it is not cached).
300    *
301    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
302    * <p class='bjava'>
303    *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map1</jv> = Map.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"1"</js>, <js>"b"</js>, <js>"2"</js>);  <jc>// size = 2</jc>
304    *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map2</jv> = Map.of(<js>"b"</js>, <js>"3"</js>, <js>"c"</js>, <js>"4"</js>);  <jc>// size = 2</jc>
305    *    MultiMap&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>multiMap</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MultiMap&lt;&gt;(<jv>map1</jv>, <jv>map2</jv>);
306    *
307    *    <jk>int</jk> <jv>totalSize</jv> = <jv>multiMap</jv>.size(); <jc>// Returns: 3 (a, b, c - b is counted only once)</jc>
308    * </p>
309    *
310    * @return The number of unique key-value mappings in this map.
311    */
312   @Override
313   public int size() {
314      var seenKeys = new HashSet<>();
315      for (var map : m) {
316         seenKeys.addAll(map.keySet());
317      }
318      return seenKeys.size();
319   }
320
321   /**
322    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this map contains no key-value mappings.
323    *
324    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this map contains no key-value mappings.
325    */
326   @Override
327   public boolean isEmpty() {
328      for (var map : m) {
329         if (!map.isEmpty())
330            return false;
331      }
332      return true;
333   }
334
335   /**
336    * Returns a {@link Collection} view of the values contained in this map.
337    *
338    * <p>
339    * The returned collection is a view of the values from the entries in {@link #entrySet()}.
340    * When a key exists in multiple maps, only the value from the first map (in constructor order)
341    * is included.
342    *
343    * @return A collection view of the values contained in this map.
344    */
345   @Override
346   public Collection<V> values() {
347      return new AbstractCollection<>() {
348         @Override
349         public Iterator<V> iterator() {
350            return new Iterator<>() {
351               private final Iterator<Entry<K,V>> entryIterator = entrySet().iterator();
352
353               @Override
354               public boolean hasNext() {
355                  return entryIterator.hasNext();
356               }
357
358               @Override
359               public V next() {
360                  return entryIterator.next().getValue();
361               }
362
363               @Override
364               public void remove() {
365                  entryIterator.remove();
366               }
367            };
368         }
369
370         @Override
371         public int size() {
372            return MultiMap.this.size();
373         }
374      };
375   }
376
377   // Unsupported operations
378   @Override
379   public V put(K key, V value) {
380      throw uoex("put() not supported on MultiMap. Use underlying maps directly.");
381   }
382
383   @Override
384   public V remove(Object key) {
385      throw uoex("remove() not supported on MultiMap. Use underlying maps directly.");
386   }
387
388   @Override
389   public void putAll(Map<? extends K, ? extends V> m) {
390      throw uoex("putAll() not supported on MultiMap. Use underlying maps directly.");
391   }
392
393   @Override
394   public void clear() {
395      throw uoex("clear() not supported on MultiMap. Use underlying maps directly.");
396   }
397
398   /**
399    * Returns a string representation of this MultiMap.
400    *
401    * <p>
402    * The format is <c>"[{...},{...},...]"</c> where each <c>{...}</c> is the standard
403    * standard string representation of each underlying map (as returned by {@link Object#toString()}).
404    *
405    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
406    * <p class='bjava'>
407    *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map1</jv> = Map.of(<js>"a"</js>, <js>"1"</js>);
408    *    Map&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>map2</jv> = Map.of(<js>"b"</js>, <js>"2"</js>);
409    *    MultiMap&lt;String, String&gt; <jv>multiMap</jv> = <jk>new</jk> MultiMap&lt;&gt;(<jv>map1</jv>, <jv>map2</jv>);
410    *    <jv>multiMap</jv>.toString(); <jc>// Returns: "[{a=1}, {b=2}]"</jc>
411    * </p>
412    *
413    * @return A string representation of this MultiMap.
414    */
415   @Override
416   public String toString() {
417      return Arrays.stream(m).map(Object::toString).collect(Collectors.joining(", ", "[", "]"));
418   }
419
420   /**
421    * Compares the specified object with this map for equality.
422    *
423    * <p>
424    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the given object is also a map and the two maps represent the same
425    * mappings. More formally, two maps <c>m1</c> and <c>m2</c> represent the same mappings if
426    * <c>m1.entrySet().equals(m2.entrySet())</c>.
427    *
428    * <p>
429    * This implementation compares the entry sets of the two maps.
430    *
431    * @param o Object to be compared for equality with this map.
432    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the specified object is equal to this map.
433    */
434   @Override
435   public boolean equals(Object o) {
436      return (o instanceof Map o2) && Shorts.eq(this, o2, (x, y) -> x.entrySet().equals(y.entrySet()));
437   }
438
439   /**
440    * Returns the hash code value for this map.
441    *
442    * <p>
443    * The hash code of a map is defined to be the sum of the hash codes of each entry in the map's
444    * <c>entrySet()</c> view. This ensures that <c>m1.equals(m2)</c> implies that
445    * <c>m1.hashCode()==m2.hashCode()</c> for any two maps <c>m1</c> and <c>m2</c>, as required
446    * by the general contract of {@link Object#hashCode()}.
447    *
448    * <p>
449    * This implementation computes the hash code from the entry set.
450    *
451    * @return The hash code value for this map.
452    */
453   @Override
454   public int hashCode() {
455      return entrySet().hashCode();
456   }
457}
458
